name: The White House
subject: ‘I don’t know if we’ll have any left to cut’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d18263276480188638ec79994bccb279eaf489e15d3e1e81edf20f8d517d9d47a99db1db09aa1cb59842eb9a4dab651f8989bb926ceb

‘I don’t know if we’ll have any left to cut’

President Donald J. Trump pulled out the ceremonial gold scissors yesterday to show Americans exactly what his Administration is doing to Washington’s vast regulatory state.

A few highlights from his speech:

- In 1960, there were 20,000 pages of federal regulations; today, there are 185,000—a stack about as tall as one of the reporters covering yesterday’s speech. (He’s 6’3”.)
- “We canceled or delayed over 1,500 planned regulatory actions, more than any previous president by far.”
- The President’s simple message for agency heads: Slash even more red tape in 2018. “And that should just about do it. I don’t know if we’ll have any left to cut.”
- One stat to remember: The Administration promised to cut 2 regulations for each new one added. Agencies blew that 2:1 goal out of the water, instead eliminating rules at a 22:1 clip.
- Why it matters: “Reductions in regulation are happening now,” financial expert Peter Wallison wrote earlier this fall. “That’s why the markets are exuberant.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d18263276480ff014e26c8a5a8e43474a19416f60d3103cdd4ccb6738ddb53b61ddc90ebd04a9f119bee94d6f466b3374a5ef528bca3
Read the President’s full remarks.

It’s a good day to celebrate

Just as the Administration unveils its plan to continue pruning the administrative state next year, it’s time to commemorate 10 of our Nation’s most important Founding principles.

President Trump signed a proclamation last week that honors today as Bill of Rights Day, part of the larger Human Rights Week that began Monday. “We recognize the importance of the first 10 Amendments to our Constitution to protecting our liberty and freedom against the inevitable encroachment of government,” the President wrote. Our Founders “understood the fundamental truth that liberty is best secured when the state’s power is carefully limited.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d1826327648013bce598de078495d839e9ddbe2c04b56eec4995a1648b51a265b9631330d9e3d9cf86e4356a838b4af75ccebf91c697
Read President Trump’s full proclamation.

The next generation of law enforcement leaders

This morning, President Trump addressed the 222 newest graduates of the FBI National Academy at a ceremony in Quantico, Virginia. He congratulated students for completing the 270th Session of the academy, a challenging 11-week professional course of study covering intelligence theory, terrorism, management, law, behavioral science, law enforcement communication, and forensic science.
Included in this latest class are members of law enforcement agencies from the District of Columbia, 20 international countries, three military organizations, and four federal civilian organizations.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d1826327648057118c801160df079aa9b584ede159b2a6a90272a0bb00d677f6f2cfa7d77977d4fd85ff90ffc62223e441a659195177
Watch the President’s remarks here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump’s official 2017 Christmas portrait | December 5, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

COMING UP

Next week, the Vice President travels to Egypt and Israel. The President will hold a meeting of his Cabinet on Wednesday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d182632764806f47429923883f073e8df76c69c213d5d2c965fc9bc5ab7a00637dbb282f0bf7a299a97772a69a29d6664035b0ebc5e9
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a0c7d182632764806bc0ae3d7b91a663de841935b9680a03cedfe8c63e41bf3c297fa77431bc2b138242273aa3f6d38fd8eb8a7349cf7857
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4cfa9ca867480a75016eff8d7cdcd7948fb28753494ff262b8ce95406885d39ec6f7fe1c257f53f0c54d883f148dfd8ca

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Happy Hanukkah
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| The time has come once again. It's time to light the candles in the menorah. Time to share a blessing (and some latkes!) with friends, family, and loved ones. And of course, time for me to extend my deepest gratitude for the wonderful work you do all year round. | []({Link})
---
**From my family to yours, have a Happy Hanukkah.**
Debbie
P.S.: No, I didn't forget our yearly tradition of sharing one of my favorite holiday recipes! Here is this year's treat: Healthy Root Vegetable Latkes. Follow me at [@cleancookingcongresswoman](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e49/622b7b5b/180e0d41/307ba47b/2331628582/VEsH/ "https://www.instagram.com/cleancookingcongresswoman/") on Instagram for more healthy food and recipes!
2 cups grated peeled sweet potato
2 cups grated peeled baking potato
1 cup grated peeled parsnip
3 ounces all-purpose flour (about 2/3 cup)
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, divided
2 large eggs
1 cup grated onion
2 tablespoons olive oil, divided
1 tablespoon chopped dill (optional)
1 cup chopped, peeled apple
3 tablespoons water
1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil
1/4 teaspoon kosher salt
1/8 teaspoon ground red pepper
Preparation
1\. Preheat oven to 325°.
2\. Place first 3 ingredients on paper towels; squeeze until barely moist. Weigh or lightly spoon flour into dry measuring cups; level with a knife. Combine flour, cumin, 1/4 teaspoon salt, eggs, and onion in a bowl; beat with a mixer at medium speed until blended. Add potato mixture; beat with a mixer at low speed until combined.
3\. Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add 2 teaspoons oil; swirl. Heap 3 tablespoons potato mixture into pan to form a patty; flatten slightly. Repeat procedure 5 times to form 6 patties. Reduce heat to medium-low; cook 6 minutes on each side or until golden brown. Place latkes on a baking sheet; keep warm in oven. Repeat procedure twice with remaining oil and potato mixture to yield 18 latkes total. Sprinkle latkes with 1/4 teaspoon salt. Garnish with dill, if desired.

name: Team Donnelly
subject: 100,000 Hoosier Children
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Peter, we're concerned. |
---

| | | Peter, we're concerned.

It's been 75 days since funding for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) expired and yet somehow, Mitch McConnell and his Republican allies haven't cared enough to renew it.

If you're not familiar with this program that provides health care for millions of American children, there's a reason why. It's so popular that for two decades, bipartisan majorities in Congress have taken action to renew it before the first sign of trouble. **But in their haste to strip 23 million Americans of their health care this summer, Republicans let insurance for children slip through the cracks in September.** And as the months tick by without action, their inattention is starting to look a lot more like outright cruelty to those too young to fight back.

**Now, we 're running out of time. CHIP provides health care for 100,000 Hoosier kids.** Joe understands how important this is for thousands of Indiana families, but the GOP is focused only on tax cuts for their special interest donors.

**Will you tell us how important it is to you that Joe continues to fight to protect Hoosier children?**

subject: The challenges that lie ahead
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |

| |
---
| **"Three Republicans have a dream of defeating Wasserman Schultz"**
\-- _Sun-Sentinel_ , 11/2017 There are three Republicans running against me. Apparently, it's their "dream" to defeat me. [_**Can you stand alongside me by giving what you can -- every dollar can make the difference.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e4e/622b7b5b/1806b5aa/307ba472/1749365361/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171215?refcode=dwse171215_m&recurring=true&amount=5")

We all know their game plan. They will attack me or spend money trying their best to defeat me. But I have to admit -- it's not their dreams I'm worried about.

I'm worried about the hardworking families in South Florida, who dream of making ends meet, building a savings, or sending their kids to college.

I'm worried about seniors, who dream of having the stability in their retirement that Medicare and Social Security provide.

I'm worried about young people, who dream of one day being able to pay off their student loans, buy a house, or start a family.

Those are the dreams I fight to make possible every single day. **And I need your help to make sure I can continue that work.**

These three Republicans will throw all their efforts into defeating me. [_**I need our help to fight back. We need to raise $5,000 in the next 24 hours if we want to stay on track for our end of quarter goal. If you can, give $5 or more to make that happen.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e4e/622b7b5b/1806b5aa/307ba472/1749365361/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse171215?refcode=dwse171215_m&recurring=true&amount=5")

Thank you,

Debbie |

name: Russ Feingold, LegitPAC
subject: Our next candidate is…
body:
[ Legit ]LegitPAC

Friend,

In response to our call, Legit members from all over the country
weighed in with suggestions for candidates to direct our resources to
-- and I really enjoyed reading through the submissions with our team.

It’s amazing to see the thought everyone put into this exercise. It
really shows why we can’t tolerate a democracy that doesn’t embrace
the participation of its people.

But ultimately, one candidate’s name, suggested by members like
Elizabeth P. in Michigan and many more, kept standing out among all
the great names: Elizabeth Warren.

Senator Warren is one of the boldest voices for the middle class we
have in Congress -- especially at a time when Republicans are pushing
to pass a tax bill almost entirely focused on providing payback to
their richest donors. That’s why, as promised when we asked for these
submissions, I’m asking you and your fellow Legit members to step up
and support Elizabeth Warren today.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/2820?t=1002&akid=462%2E356062%2Eye-rUo ]Support a leader suggested by your fellow Legit members now:
Elizabeth Warren. Contribute $3 to Senator Warren today.

Senator Warren works tirelessly in the fight against corruption in
Washington. She has pushed for legislation like the Fair Elections Now
Act, the DISCLOSE Act, and the Shareholder Protection Act, and she’s
been a leader on investigating conflicts of interest with the Trump
administration.

Senator Warren has also worked tirelessly to support progressive
candidates throughout the country, including helping with fundraising
for my Senate race last year and even joining me on the campaign
trail.

I founded LegitPAC to support candidates who will fight for policies
to restore legitimacy to our government. And I can safely say that
Senator Warren was one of the best suggestions Legit members made.

At a time when every bit of progress we can make matters, it’s vitally
important we come together today and support Senator Warren.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/2820?t=1003&akid=462%2E356062%2Eye-rUo ]Step up to support Senator Warren at this vital time for progress.
Contribute $3
today.

I’m grateful to everyone who took the time to submit a suggestion.
With Republicans set to pass their thoroughly corrupt tax bill, it’s
clear how important it is for us to support leaders who will fight for
everyday American voters in Congress. And we can’t select a better
leader for legitimacy than Elizabeth Warren.

Thanks for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitPAC

Yesterday, the FCC voted to destroy net neutrality.

That means that large internet companies can now slow down your internet or block certain sites that can't afford to pay for faster speeds.

This is outrageous. The internet should be free and open for all -- not based on a pay-for-play system. But it's not too late for Congress to act.

[**Add your name to tell Republicans to overturn this decision and return to the free and fair system that should govern our internet >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d065/2b07114c/ab163bb6/73193b9d/2345346584/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-net-neutrality?source=PT-20171215-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Big corporations will see soaring profits, while small start-ups will never have the ability to get off the ground. This decision is a major blow to innovation. It threatens free speech too, by allowing internet providers to block any content that goes against their political agenda.

**Every week we feature a “Republican of the Week,” and this week the honor goes to Speaker Gene Chandler.**
****
**Speaker Chandler has not missed a beat since taking on the leadership position in the House. His focus is communication, unity and success for House Republicans.**
****
**This week Chandler announced an expanded leadership team and the formation of a Policy Advisory Committee. True to his word, Chandler pledged to keep the current leadership. But he also promised to add to the leadership team and bring more House members on, and that’s exactly what he did. I especially appreciate his desire to want as many opinions and voices at the table as possible. clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, DECEMBER 15TH | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Every week we feature a “Republican of the Week,” and this week the honor goes to Speaker Gene Chandler. ** Speaker Chandler has not missed a beat since taking on the leadership position in the House. His focus is communication, unity and success for House Republicans. This week Chandler announced an expanded leadership team and the formation of a Policy Advisory Committee. True to his word, Chandler pledged to keep the current leadership. But he also promised to add to the leadership team and bring more House members on, and that’s exactly what he did. I especially appreciate his desire to want as many opinions and voices at the table as possible. As he said in his recent press release, “The message coming out of the caucus and election in November was one of unity...and in order to have a productive and successful legislative session, we must be unified in order to prove to our constituents that we deserve their vote next November.” To see the release, Speaker Chandler that will be chaired by Rep. Laurie Sanborn with Rep. David Bates as vice chair. The committee will meet weekly to review upcoming legislation and provide input to the Speaker. This kind of proactive action assures House Republicans will be working together for a productive session. Also this week, Chandler announced the formation of an Elections and Fundraising Committee to provide advice to the House Republican majority. This group will provide advice on fundraising and candidate recruitment to assure success in 2018. Under Chandler’s leadership, House Republicans are poised to achieve some great successes in the upcoming session! Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle!
